validation.rationale: Authoritative spec is the user-reviewed RCA at `docs/feature/fix-recvmsg4-reply-source-port/deliver/rca.md` (verdict: genuine defect, ADR-0053 §D4 directs addr+port rewrite, current code writes addr only). Two-step Outside-In TDD encoding exactly the RCA fix plan items 1-6, no scope expansion. Mirrors the canonical bug-fix shape from sibling fix-terminal-reason-channel-closed/deliver/roadmap.json and fix-exit-observer-write-retry/deliver/roadmap.json: RED regression step then single-cut GREEN production fix, no fallback, no feature-flag retention, no same-port interim mitigation.

notes: Bug fix via /nw-bugfix -> /nw-deliver. Authoritative spec is the RCA at `docs/feature/fix-recvmsg4-reply-source-port/deliver/rca.md` (user-reviewed, verdict = genuine defect). The roadmap encodes exactly the RCA fix plan (items 1-6) as a 2-step TDD sequence. Do NOT expand scope. Do NOT redesign.

One-line bug: `cgroup_recvmsg4_service` reverse source-rewrite restores only the source IP (`user_ip4` from VIP) and drops the source PORT. For cross-port services (DNS VIP:53 -> backend:5353) the app's `recvfrom` sees `(VIP, backend_port)` and source-validating resolvers (Unbound, BIND 9) reject the reply. The reverse map value (`REVERSE_LOCAL_MAP`) is a bare `u32` (VIP IP only), so there is no VIP port to write back. Fix = widen the reverse value to carry the VIP port and rewrite `user_port` too.

OOP paradigm; crafter is @nw-software-crafter. This is a kernel-side correctness fix with NO Tier-2 backstop: `BPF_PROG_TEST_RUN` returns ENOTSUPP for `cgroup_sock_addr` programs on kernel <= 6.8, so the PKTGEN/SETUP/CHECK triptych cannot exercise a synthetic `bpf_sock_addr` ctx. The Tier-3 round-trip (`unconnected_udp_roundtrip.rs`) is the only behavioural gate for the kernel program; the Tier-1 Sim mirror + lockstep invariant is the structural defense. Both must be Lima-verified in the same PR. See MEMORY `feedback_no_tier2_ebpf_hook_firing_scope_needs_tier3_spike`.

The `user_port` write idiom is load-bearing: `user_port = u32::from(vip_port_host.to_be())` (the low-16-NBO idiom). Do NOT write `u32::from_be(...) as u16` or `from_be(...) as u16` — that is the silent-0 trap documented in `.claude/rules/development.md` (`### bpf_sock_addr.user_port — low-16-NBO in a u32`). `cgroup_sendmsg4_service.rs:90` is the correct precedent for both the read and the write side.

Endianness lockstep (`.claude/rules/development.md` § 'Userspace map insertion -> Endianness lockstep'): userspace stores host-order bytes (`vip_host`, `vip_port_host`); the kernel-side program converts to network-order at the read/write boundary. The userspace handle never byte-flips. The extended host-order roundtrip proptest pins this for both `vip_host` and the new `vip_port_host` field plus the 8-byte value layout.

Byte-parity guard: add an `assert!(size_of::<...>() == 8)` on BOTH the kernel-side `ReverseLocalEntry` struct AND the userspace `ReverseLocalEntryPod`. The value width grows 4 -> 8 bytes; the existing 8-byte guard is on the *key* (`ReverseLocalKey`), so this is a *new* value-side assert, not a parity break. Risk is LOW.

No rkyv/redb schema-evolution fixture is needed: `REVERSE_LOCAL_MAP` is a live BPF map recreated from intent on boot, not an rkyv-persisted type. Migration policy is single-cut greenfield (delete the live map; it is rebuilt on next register).

DECOMPOSITION RATIO IS INTENTIONALLY HIGH (files/step > 2.5). This is a single byte-layout change (reverse map value u32 -> 8-byte struct) rippling across kernel ELF + userspace handle + dual-write call site + Sim mirror + Tier-1 invariant + two Tier-3 test files in lockstep. The fan-out is inherent to the lockstep boundary, not a decomposition defect. The quality-gate ratio flag on this roadmap is EXPECTED and should not be treated as a finding — splitting the layout change across more steps would leave the kernel and userspace on mismatched value widths between commits, which does not compile and cannot be Lima-verified.

Reverse-first dual-write ordering is preserved. `register_local_backend` at `crates/overdrive-dataplane/src/lib.rs` upserts REVERSE first (~:2089) then FORWARD second (~:2097). Only the reverse value payload widens; the ordering is unchanged. `vip_port` is ALREADY in scope at the call site (`register_local_backend(vip, vip_port, backend, proto)`, ~:2081-2087).

OUT OF SCOPE — ADR-0053 D1/D4 reconciliation. The RCA fix-plan item 7 (reconcile the intra-ADR contradiction: D1 value schema is IP-only `u32` while D4 rewrite directive is addr+port) is routed SEPARATELY through @nw-solution-architect per the project rule that ADR edits go via the architect agent, never inline. This roadmap contains NO ADR edit step. Do not add one.

OUT OF SCOPE for this feature (do not address): the forward path (`sendmsg4`/`connect4` already do full addr+port NAT, correct as-is); the non-service recvmsg4 path (must leave the REAL sender's source intact — guarded by the non-service assertion at `unconnected_udp_reply_hardening.rs:263`); any other map or program. The fix is bounded to the reverse-local source-rewrite value schema and its `recvmsg4` consumer.

TEST PARADIGM context: the userspace handle roundtrip is property-based (proptest) per `.claude/rules/testing.md` § 'newtype roundtrip is mandatory' — extend the existing host-order roundtrip proptest. The Tier-3 round-trip and Tier-1 lockstep are single-example/invariant tests verifying wiring and a cross-method observable invariant respectively; they are not amenable to @given amplification (they assert a concrete cross-port fixture and a lockstep equality). The kernel-side program has no unit-test surface (no Tier-2).

phases.name: Bug fix — recvmsg4 reverse source-rewrite restores VIP (addr, port), not addr only
user_stories: As an operator running a same-host unconnected-UDP service whose VIP port differs from the backend port (canonical: DNS `VIP:53 -> backend:5353`), when my application calls `recvfrom` on the reply socket it sees source `(VIP_IP, VIP_PORT)` — NOT `(VIP_IP, backend_port)`. Source-validating resolvers (Unbound, BIND 9) accept the reply instead of silently discarding it, so the cross-port DNS service works end to end.
phases.description: Widen the reverse-local map value from a bare `u32` (VIP IP only) to an 8-byte `(vip_host: u32, vip_port_host: u16, _pad: u16)` POD carrying both the VIP address and the VIP port, in lockstep across the kernel ELF, the userspace handle, the dual-write call site, and the Sim adapter. Make `cgroup_recvmsg4_service` rewrite `user_port` (low-16-NBO idiom) in addition to `user_ip4`, so the reverse path performs full `(addr, port)` translation symmetric with the forward path. The fix is single-cut: the value schema and every consumer move together in one PR; reverse-first dual-write ordering is preserved.

steps.name: RED — assert reply source PORT == VIP_PORT in Tier-3 round-trip and Tier-1 lockstep; widen the Sim mirror to carry a port
steps.description: Land the failing regression surface and the Sim-side structural change WITHOUT touching the kernel ELF or the userspace handle value width (those are step 01-02). Five edits: (1) In `crates/overdrive-dataplane/tests/integration/unconnected_udp_roundtrip.rs` add an `assert_eq!(src.port(), VIP_PORT, ...)` alongside each existing reply-source IP assertion (the IP checks are at L221, L309, L324, L408; VIP=10.96.0.10 at L68, VIP_PORT=53 at L73; backend forced off :53/:5353 at L196-197, so the cross-port shape is already exercised). These assertions FAIL against current code because recvmsg4 leaves the source port = backend port. (2) In `crates/overdrive-dataplane/tests/integration/unconnected_udp_reply_hardening.rs` add a port assertion at the SERVICE-reply check (L307) asserting the service reply source port == VIP_PORT; at the NON-service check (L263) assert the REAL sender's source port stays intact (the non-service path must NOT be rewritten). (3) In `crates/overdrive-sim/src/adapters/dataplane.rs` widen `reply_mirror` from `BTreeMap<BackendKey, Ipv4Addr>` (L135) to `BTreeMap<BackendKey, SocketAddrV4>`, change `reply_source_for(&self, key) -> Option<Ipv4Addr>` (L199-200) to `-> Option<SocketAddrV4>`, and change the write site at L515 to store `SocketAddrV4::new(vip, vip_port)` (the Sim `register_local_backend` already receives `vip_port`). (4) In `crates/overdrive-sim/src/invariants/reply_source_rewrite_lockstep.rs` build the expected value at L213 as `SocketAddrV4::new(vip, vip_port)` and change the assertion at L218-219 to compare `reply_source_for(*key) == Some(*expected)` on the full `(IP, port)`. The Tier-1 lockstep now FAILS structurally because the current Sim cannot represent a port and the production kernel does not write one. (5) Apply RED-scaffold discipline: the Tier-3 port assertions are RED until the kernel value+program change in 01-02; commit with the documented RED-scaffold escape per `.claude/rules/testing.md` § 'RED scaffolds and intentionally-failing commits'. Commit marker `test(dataplane,sim): RED — reply source port == VIP_PORT (cross-port reverse rewrite)`. Note: widening the Sim mirror is a real production-of-test-double change and compiles cleanly on its own; only the Tier-3 kernel-path assertions are intentionally RED.
criteria: AC1: `crates/overdrive-dataplane/tests/integration/unconnected_udp_roundtrip.rs` asserts `src.port() == VIP_PORT` at every reply-source assertion site (alongside the existing IP checks near L221, L309, L324, L408). VIP_PORT (53, from L73) is the expected source port; the backend listens off :53/:5353 (L196-197), so a passing assertion proves cross-port reverse translation.

AC2: `crates/overdrive-dataplane/tests/integration/unconnected_udp_reply_hardening.rs`: the SERVICE-reply check (near L307) asserts source port == VIP_PORT; the NON-service check (near L263) asserts the REAL sender's source port is unchanged (no rewrite on the non-service path).

AC3: `crates/overdrive-sim/src/adapters/dataplane.rs`: `reply_mirror` value type is `SocketAddrV4` (was `Ipv4Addr` at L135); `reply_source_for` returns `Option<SocketAddrV4>` (was `Option<Ipv4Addr>` at L199-200); the write site at L515 stores `SocketAddrV4::new(vip, vip_port)`.

AC4: `crates/overdrive-sim/src/invariants/reply_source_rewrite_lockstep.rs`: the expected value (built near L213) is `SocketAddrV4::new(vip, vip_port)`; the assertion (near L218-219) compares the full `(IP, port)` via `reply_source_for(*key) == Some(*expected)`.

AC5: The Tier-1 lockstep invariant and the Tier-3 round-trip port assertions FAIL against current production code (recvmsg4 writes only `user_ip4`; the reverse map carries no port). The failure messages name the gap (e.g. 'reply source port: expected 53, got <backend_port>').

AC6: The Sim-side changes (AC3, AC4) compile cleanly — widening a test-double value type and an invariant is a self-contained, non-RED change. Only the kernel-path Tier-3 assertions (AC1, AC2 service-side) are intentionally RED pending 01-02.

AC7: Default lane: `cargo nextest run -p overdrive-sim` and `cargo nextest run -p overdrive-dataplane --no-run` compile; the Tier-1 lockstep is the only new sim-side failure and fails with the documented `(IP, port)` mismatch.
AC8: macOS compile-only gate: `cargo nextest run --workspace --features integration-tests --no-run` succeeds (the Sim type widening does not break any cfg-gated surface).
AC9: Commit shape: RED-scaffold escape per testing.md; commit marker `test(dataplane,sim): RED — reply source port == VIP_PORT (cross-port reverse rewrite)`.
acceptance_criteria: AC1: `crates/overdrive-dataplane/tests/integration/unconnected_udp_roundtrip.rs` asserts `src.port() == VIP_PORT` at every reply-source assertion site (alongside the existing IP checks near L221, L309, L324, L408). VIP_PORT (53, from L73) is the expected source port; the backend listens off :53/:5353 (L196-197), so a passing assertion proves cross-port reverse translation.

AC2: `crates/overdrive-dataplane/tests/integration/unconnected_udp_reply_hardening.rs`: the SERVICE-reply check (near L307) asserts source port == VIP_PORT; the NON-service check (near L263) asserts the REAL sender's source port is unchanged (no rewrite on the non-service path).

AC3: `crates/overdrive-sim/src/adapters/dataplane.rs`: `reply_mirror` value type is `SocketAddrV4` (was `Ipv4Addr` at L135); `reply_source_for` returns `Option<SocketAddrV4>` (was `Option<Ipv4Addr>` at L199-200); the write site at L515 stores `SocketAddrV4::new(vip, vip_port)`.

AC4: `crates/overdrive-sim/src/invariants/reply_source_rewrite_lockstep.rs`: the expected value (built near L213) is `SocketAddrV4::new(vip, vip_port)`; the assertion (near L218-219) compares the full `(IP, port)` via `reply_source_for(*key) == Some(*expected)`.

AC5: The Tier-1 lockstep invariant and the Tier-3 round-trip port assertions FAIL against current production code (recvmsg4 writes only `user_ip4`; the reverse map carries no port). The failure messages name the gap (e.g. 'reply source port: expected 53, got <backend_port>').

AC6: The Sim-side changes (AC3, AC4) compile cleanly — widening a test-double value type and an invariant is a self-contained, non-RED change. Only the kernel-path Tier-3 assertions (AC1, AC2 service-side) are intentionally RED pending 01-02.

AC7: Default lane: `cargo nextest run -p overdrive-sim` and `cargo nextest run -p overdrive-dataplane --no-run` compile; the Tier-1 lockstep is the only new sim-side failure and fails with the documented `(IP, port)` mismatch.
AC8: macOS compile-only gate: `cargo nextest run --workspace --features integration-tests --no-run` succeeds (the Sim type widening does not break any cfg-gated surface).
AC9: Commit shape: RED-scaffold escape per testing.md; commit marker `test(dataplane,sim): RED — reply source port == VIP_PORT (cross-port reverse rewrite)`.
steps.scenario_name: reply source port equals VIP port (cross-port reverse rewrite)
quality_gates: Default lane: `cargo nextest run -p overdrive-sim` — the new Tier-1 reply-source-rewrite lockstep is the only new failure, with the documented `(IP, port)` mismatch message.

Compile-check: `cargo xtask lima run -- cargo check -p overdrive-dataplane --tests --features integration-tests` (or macOS `cargo nextest run -p overdrive-dataplane --features integration-tests --no-run`) — the new Tier-3 port assertions compile.
macOS compile-only: `cargo nextest run --workspace --features integration-tests --no-run` succeeds.

Commit shape: RED-scaffold escape per `.claude/rules/testing.md` § 'RED scaffolds and intentionally-failing commits'; commit marker `test(dataplane,sim): RED — reply source port == VIP_PORT (cross-port reverse rewrite)`.
deliverables: One commit landing the Tier-3 source-port assertions (both round-trip and reply-hardening), the widened Sim `reply_mirror`/`reply_source_for` value type, and the `(IP, port)` lockstep invariant. The kernel-path assertions are RED pending 01-02; the Sim/invariant change is GREEN structural defense.

production: crates/overdrive-sim/src/adapters/dataplane.rs (widen `reply_mirror` value Ipv4Addr -> SocketAddrV4; `reply_source_for` -> Option<SocketAddrV4>; store SocketAddrV4::new(vip, vip_port) at write site) — sim adapter (test double), not runtime production

steps.implementation_notes: Kernel-side correctness fix with NO Tier-2 backstop (BPF_PROG_TEST_RUN ENOTSUPP for cgroup_sock_addr <= 6.8) — the Tier-3 round-trip is the only behavioural gate for the kernel program, the Tier-1 Sim lockstep is the structural defense. The Tier-3 source-port assertions are RED until the kernel value+program change lands in step 01-02; apply RED-scaffold discipline (the documented `--no-verify` escape per testing.md). The Sim mirror widening (Ipv4Addr -> SocketAddrV4) and the (IP, port) lockstep invariant are self-contained and compile/pass on their own — they are the Tier-1 structural defense that the production kernel fix in 01-02 must satisfy. EXEMPT FROM PARADIGM (Tier-3/Tier-1): the round-trip verifies end-to-end wiring against a concrete cross-port fixture (VIP:53 -> backend off :5353) and the lockstep asserts a cross-method observable equality — neither is a @given-amplifiable property; integration and invariant tests remain single-example/single-invariant per `.claude/rules/testing.md`. Compensate with the property-based host-order roundtrip proptest on the userspace handle in step 01-02. Do NOT widen the kernel ELF value or the userspace handle in this step — that is 01-02; keeping them on the old 4-byte width here is what makes the Tier-3 assertions RED. DECOMPOSITION: this step touches 1 sim-adapter file + 1 invariant + 2 test files (ratio > 2.5 vs production); the fan-out is the lockstep boundary (a single source-port concept observed by the Sim mirror, the invariant, and two Tier-3 oracles), not a decomposition defect — the ratio flag is EXPECTED.

steps.name: GREEN — widen reverse-local map value to (vip, vip_port); recvmsg4 rewrites user_port (low-16-NBO); dual-write passes vip_port
steps.description: Land the single-cut production fix that flips the step-01-01 RED assertions to GREEN, in lockstep across the kernel ELF and userspace. Four edits: (1) Kernel map value `crates/overdrive-bpf/src/maps/reverse_local_map.rs`: change `REVERSE_LOCAL_MAP` value (L71) from `u32` to a `#[repr(C)]` `ReverseLocalEntry { vip_host: u32, vip_port_host: u16, _pad: u16 }` (8-byte POD); add `const _: () = assert!(core::mem::size_of::<ReverseLocalEntry>() == 8);`. The key (`ReverseLocalKey`, L43-55, 8-byte) is UNCHANGED. (2) Kernel program `crates/overdrive-bpf/src/programs/cgroup_recvmsg4_service.rs`: in the HIT rewrite block (L144-159) read `entry.vip_host` and `entry.vip_port_host` from the new value; write `(*sock_addr).user_ip4 = entry.vip_host.to_be()` (L157, addr — unchanged semantics) AND `(*sock_addr).user_port = u32::from(entry.vip_port_host.to_be())` (the low-16-NBO idiom — do NOT use `from_be(...) as u16`, the silent-0 trap). Update the deliberate comment at L151-155 (it currently explains why the port is left alone) and the module docstring at L57-59 to state the reverse path now restores both addr and port. `cgroup_sendmsg4_service.rs:90` is the precedent for the NBO write idiom. (3) Userspace handle `crates/overdrive-dataplane/src/maps/reverse_local_map_handle.rs`: replace the bare-`u32` value on the `HashMap` (L113) with a new `#[repr(C)]` `ReverseLocalEntryPod { vip_host: u32, vip_port_host: u16, _pad: u16 }` deriving `aya::Pod`, with an `assert!(size_of == 8)`; change the codec `ReverseLocalMapValue::encode(vip, vip_port) -> ReverseLocalEntryPod` / `decode(ReverseLocalEntryPod) -> (Ipv4Addr, u16)` (was encode(vip)->u32 / decode(u32)->Ipv4Addr, L99-108); add `vip_port` to `upsert(backend_ip, backend_port, proto, vip, vip_port)` (L130-140); update `entries() -> Vec<(ReverseLocalKeyPod, ReverseLocalEntryPod)>` (L189); extend the host-order roundtrip proptest (L237-292) to pin `vip_port_host` and the 8-byte value byte layout. (4) Dual-write call site + probe `crates/overdrive-dataplane/src/lib.rs`: pass `vip_port` into the REVERSE upsert call (L2089 — `vip_port` is already in scope from `register_local_backend(vip, vip_port, backend, proto)` at L2081-2087); the reverse-first / forward-second ordering (L2089 then L2097) is PRESERVED. Update `probe_reverse_local` (L1207-1262) to write a sentinel value with `vip_port = 0` and read it back via the new `entries()` shape; update `reverse_local_sentinel_verdict` for the new value shape. Run mutation testing on the diff. The step-01-01 RED assertions (Tier-3 source port == VIP_PORT, Tier-1 lockstep (IP, port)) transition GREEN; all existing tests still pass. Standard commit (no `--no-verify`). Commit marker `fix(dataplane): recvmsg4 reverse rewrite restores VIP (addr, port), not addr only`.
criteria: AC1: `crates/overdrive-bpf/src/maps/reverse_local_map.rs`: `REVERSE_LOCAL_MAP` value type is `#[repr(C)]` `ReverseLocalEntry { vip_host: u32, vip_port_host: u16, _pad: u16 }` (was `u32` at L71). A `const _: () = assert!(core::mem::size_of::<ReverseLocalEntry>() == 8);` guards the 8-byte layout. `ReverseLocalKey` is unchanged.

AC2: `crates/overdrive-bpf/src/programs/cgroup_recvmsg4_service.rs`: the HIT rewrite block (L144-159) writes `(*sock_addr).user_ip4 = entry.vip_host.to_be()` AND `(*sock_addr).user_port = u32::from(entry.vip_port_host.to_be())`. The write uses the low-16-NBO idiom (`u32::from(<u16>.to_be())`), NOT `from_be(...) as u16`. The L151-155 comment and the L57-59 module docstring are updated to state the reverse path restores both addr and port.

AC3: `crates/overdrive-dataplane/src/maps/reverse_local_map_handle.rs`: the `HashMap` value (L113) is a new `#[repr(C)]` `ReverseLocalEntryPod { vip_host: u32, vip_port_host: u16, _pad: u16 }` deriving `aya::Pod`, with an 8-byte size assert. The codec `encode(vip, vip_port)` / `decode -> (Ipv4Addr, u16)`, `upsert(.., vip, vip_port)`, and `entries() -> Vec<(ReverseLocalKeyPod, ReverseLocalEntryPod)>` carry the VIP port (was IP-only at L99-108, L130-140, L189).

AC4: The host-order roundtrip proptest in `reverse_local_map_handle.rs` (L237-292) is extended to generate and assert `vip_port_host` round-trips host-order through `encode`/`decode`, AND pins the 8-byte value byte layout. Existing `vip_host` roundtrip cases continue to pass.

AC5: `crates/overdrive-dataplane/src/lib.rs`: the REVERSE upsert call (L2089) passes `vip_port` (already in scope from `register_local_backend`). The reverse-first (L2089) then forward-second (L2097) dual-write ordering is unchanged.

AC6: `crates/overdrive-dataplane/src/lib.rs`: `probe_reverse_local` (L1207-1262) writes a sentinel value with `vip_port = 0` and reads it back through the new `entries()` value shape; `reverse_local_sentinel_verdict` is updated for the 8-byte value. The probe (wire-then-probe Earned-Trust gate) still passes on a healthy map.

AC7: Byte-parity: an `assert!(size_of == 8)` exists on BOTH the kernel-side `ReverseLocalEntry` (AC1) and the userspace `ReverseLocalEntryPod` (AC3). The value layout matches across the kernel/userspace boundary (verified by the extended roundtrip proptest layout assertion).

AC8: Tier-1: the `reply_source_rewrite_lockstep` invariant transitions RED -> GREEN — the Sim mirror's `SocketAddrV4` and the production reverse value's `(vip, vip_port)` now agree on the full `(IP, port)`.

AC9: Tier-3 (Lima): `unconnected_udp_roundtrip.rs` and `unconnected_udp_reply_hardening.rs` source-port assertions transition RED -> GREEN. The cross-port DNS fixture (VIP:53 -> backend off :5353) now produces a reply whose source is `(VIP_IP, 53)`. Verified via `cargo xtask lima run -- cargo nextest run -p overdrive-dataplane --features integration-tests` (kernel-path correctness has no macOS/Tier-2 substitute).

AC10: Existing tests pass unchanged: the non-service reply-hardening path (real sender source intact), the forward-path NAT tests, and every other `overdrive-dataplane` / `overdrive-bpf` / `overdrive-sim` test. The value-width change is additive at the layout level (4 -> 8 bytes on a live BPF map, not an rkyv-persisted type — no schema-evolution fixture required).
AC11: `cargo xtask bpf-build` succeeds and emits `overdrive_bpf.o` with the recompiled `cgroup_recvmsg4_service` program (the BPF object is a prerequisite for the dataplane build/test surface).

AC12: Default lane: `cargo nextest run -p overdrive-sim`, `cargo nextest run -p overdrive-dataplane` (unit/proptest), and the workspace lane PASS. macOS compile-only: `cargo nextest run --workspace --features integration-tests --no-run` PASSES.

AC13: Mutation testing kill rate >= 80% on the diff via `cargo xtask lima run -- cargo xtask mutants --diff origin/main --features integration-tests --package overdrive-dataplane`. The mutation surface is the codec (`encode`/`decode`), the `upsert`/`entries` value plumbing, and the sentinel verdict. The kernel-side ELF program is EXCLUDED from mutants per `.claude/rules/testing.md` (aya-rs eBPF programs are not mutated). If kill rate < 80%, surface the missed mutations and add tests or document unreachability.
AC14: Commit shape: standard `git commit` (no `--no-verify`). Commit marker `fix(dataplane): recvmsg4 reverse rewrite restores VIP (addr, port), not addr only`. Body cites the RCA path.
acceptance_criteria: AC1: `crates/overdrive-bpf/src/maps/reverse_local_map.rs`: `REVERSE_LOCAL_MAP` value type is `#[repr(C)]` `ReverseLocalEntry { vip_host: u32, vip_port_host: u16, _pad: u16 }` (was `u32` at L71). A `const _: () = assert!(core::mem::size_of::<ReverseLocalEntry>() == 8);` guards the 8-byte layout. `ReverseLocalKey` is unchanged.

AC2: `crates/overdrive-bpf/src/programs/cgroup_recvmsg4_service.rs`: the HIT rewrite block (L144-159) writes `(*sock_addr).user_ip4 = entry.vip_host.to_be()` AND `(*sock_addr).user_port = u32::from(entry.vip_port_host.to_be())`. The write uses the low-16-NBO idiom (`u32::from(<u16>.to_be())`), NOT `from_be(...) as u16`. The L151-155 comment and the L57-59 module docstring are updated to state the reverse path restores both addr and port.

AC3: `crates/overdrive-dataplane/src/maps/reverse_local_map_handle.rs`: the `HashMap` value (L113) is a new `#[repr(C)]` `ReverseLocalEntryPod { vip_host: u32, vip_port_host: u16, _pad: u16 }` deriving `aya::Pod`, with an 8-byte size assert. The codec `encode(vip, vip_port)` / `decode -> (Ipv4Addr, u16)`, `upsert(.., vip, vip_port)`, and `entries() -> Vec<(ReverseLocalKeyPod, ReverseLocalEntryPod)>` carry the VIP port (was IP-only at L99-108, L130-140, L189).

AC4: The host-order roundtrip proptest in `reverse_local_map_handle.rs` (L237-292) is extended to generate and assert `vip_port_host` round-trips host-order through `encode`/`decode`, AND pins the 8-byte value byte layout. Existing `vip_host` roundtrip cases continue to pass.

AC5: `crates/overdrive-dataplane/src/lib.rs`: the REVERSE upsert call (L2089) passes `vip_port` (already in scope from `register_local_backend`). The reverse-first (L2089) then forward-second (L2097) dual-write ordering is unchanged.

AC6: `crates/overdrive-dataplane/src/lib.rs`: `probe_reverse_local` (L1207-1262) writes a sentinel value with `vip_port = 0` and reads it back through the new `entries()` value shape; `reverse_local_sentinel_verdict` is updated for the 8-byte value. The probe (wire-then-probe Earned-Trust gate) still passes on a healthy map.

AC7: Byte-parity: an `assert!(size_of == 8)` exists on BOTH the kernel-side `ReverseLocalEntry` (AC1) and the userspace `ReverseLocalEntryPod` (AC3). The value layout matches across the kernel/userspace boundary (verified by the extended roundtrip proptest layout assertion).

AC8: Tier-1: the `reply_source_rewrite_lockstep` invariant transitions RED -> GREEN — the Sim mirror's `SocketAddrV4` and the production reverse value's `(vip, vip_port)` now agree on the full `(IP, port)`.

AC9: Tier-3 (Lima): `unconnected_udp_roundtrip.rs` and `unconnected_udp_reply_hardening.rs` source-port assertions transition RED -> GREEN. The cross-port DNS fixture (VIP:53 -> backend off :5353) now produces a reply whose source is `(VIP_IP, 53)`. Verified via `cargo xtask lima run -- cargo nextest run -p overdrive-dataplane --features integration-tests` (kernel-path correctness has no macOS/Tier-2 substitute).

AC10: Existing tests pass unchanged: the non-service reply-hardening path (real sender source intact), the forward-path NAT tests, and every other `overdrive-dataplane` / `overdrive-bpf` / `overdrive-sim` test. The value-width change is additive at the layout level (4 -> 8 bytes on a live BPF map, not an rkyv-persisted type — no schema-evolution fixture required).
AC11: `cargo xtask bpf-build` succeeds and emits `overdrive_bpf.o` with the recompiled `cgroup_recvmsg4_service` program (the BPF object is a prerequisite for the dataplane build/test surface).

AC12: Default lane: `cargo nextest run -p overdrive-sim`, `cargo nextest run -p overdrive-dataplane` (unit/proptest), and the workspace lane PASS. macOS compile-only: `cargo nextest run --workspace --features integration-tests --no-run` PASSES.

AC13: Mutation testing kill rate >= 80% on the diff via `cargo xtask lima run -- cargo xtask mutants --diff origin/main --features integration-tests --package overdrive-dataplane`. The mutation surface is the codec (`encode`/`decode`), the `upsert`/`entries` value plumbing, and the sentinel verdict. The kernel-side ELF program is EXCLUDED from mutants per `.claude/rules/testing.md` (aya-rs eBPF programs are not mutated). If kill rate < 80%, surface the missed mutations and add tests or document unreachability.
AC14: Commit shape: standard `git commit` (no `--no-verify`). Commit marker `fix(dataplane): recvmsg4 reverse rewrite restores VIP (addr, port), not addr only`. Body cites the RCA path.
steps.scenario_name: reverse map value carries VIP port; recvmsg4 rewrites user_port
quality_gates: BPF build: `cargo xtask bpf-build` emits `overdrive_bpf.o` with the recompiled `cgroup_recvmsg4_service`.
Default lane: `cargo nextest run -p overdrive-sim` (lockstep invariant GREEN) and `cargo nextest run -p overdrive-dataplane` (handle roundtrip proptest GREEN) PASS.

Tier-3 (Lima): `cargo xtask lima run -- cargo nextest run -p overdrive-dataplane --features integration-tests` — the round-trip and reply-hardening source-port assertions PASS (no macOS/Tier-2 substitute for kernel-path correctness).
Workspace lane: `cargo nextest run --workspace` PASSES; doctests `cargo test --doc --workspace` PASS.
macOS compile-only: `cargo nextest run --workspace --features integration-tests --no-run` PASSES.

Mutation testing: kill rate >= 80% on the diff via `cargo xtask lima run -- cargo xtask mutants --diff origin/main --features integration-tests --package overdrive-dataplane` (kernel-side ELF excluded per testing.md).
Commit shape: standard `git commit` (no `--no-verify`); commit marker `fix(dataplane): recvmsg4 reverse rewrite restores VIP (addr, port), not addr only`.
deliverables: One atomic commit landing the reverse-local value widening (kernel ELF + userspace POD, 8-byte parity asserts on both), the recvmsg4 `user_port` low-16-NBO rewrite, the dual-write `vip_port` plumbing (reverse-first ordering preserved), the probe/sentinel update, and the extended host-order roundtrip proptest. Step-01-01 RED assertions transition GREEN; mutation kill rate >= 80%.

production: crates/overdrive-bpf/src/maps/reverse_local_map.rs (value u32 -> 8-byte ReverseLocalEntry; size assert; key unchanged)
crates/overdrive-bpf/src/programs/cgroup_recvmsg4_service.rs (read vip_host + vip_port_host; write user_ip4 + user_port low-16-NBO; update comment + docstring)
crates/overdrive-dataplane/src/maps/reverse_local_map_handle.rs (ReverseLocalEntryPod 8-byte aya::Pod; codec/upsert/entries gain vip_port; extend roundtrip proptest)
crates/overdrive-dataplane/src/lib.rs (reverse upsert passes vip_port; probe_reverse_local + sentinel verdict for new value shape; reverse-first ordering preserved)
tests: crates/overdrive-dataplane/src/maps/reverse_local_map_handle.rs (host-order roundtrip proptest extended to pin vip_port_host + 8-byte value layout — co-located with the handle)

steps.implementation_notes: Single-cut production fix; the kernel ELF value width and the userspace POD value width MUST move together in one commit (mismatched widths do not compile and cannot be Lima-verified) — this is why the decomposition fans across 4 production files. The user_port write idiom `u32::from(vip_port_host.to_be())` (low-16-NBO) is load-bearing; `from_be(...) as u16` is the silent-0 trap documented in `.claude/rules/development.md` (`### bpf_sock_addr.user_port`). `cgroup_sendmsg4_service.rs:90` is the correct precedent. Endianness lockstep: userspace stores host-order (`vip_host`, `vip_port_host`), kernel converts to NBO at the boundary; the handle never flips. Byte-parity: 8-byte size assert on BOTH the kernel struct and the userspace POD. No rkyv schema-evolution fixture — REVERSE_LOCAL_MAP is a live BPF map rebuilt from intent on boot, not an rkyv/redb-persisted type; single-cut greenfield migration. Reverse-first dual-write ordering at lib.rs (~L2089 reverse then ~L2097 forward) is PRESERVED — only the value payload widens. TEST PARADIGM: the userspace handle roundtrip is property-based (proptest @ host-order roundtrip is mandatory per `.claude/rules/testing.md` newtype-roundtrip rule) — extend, do not replace. EXEMPT FROM PARADIGM (kernel program): the eBPF program has no unit-test surface (Tier-2 BPF_PROG_TEST_RUN is ENOTSUPP for cgroup_sock_addr <= 6.8); its correctness is gated solely by the Tier-3 round-trip. Compensate with the host-order roundtrip proptest on the userspace value codec (this step) and the (IP, port) lockstep invariant (step 01-01). DECOMPOSITION: 4 production files for one byte-layout change (ratio > 2.5); the fan-out is the kernel/userspace lockstep boundary, not a decomposition defect — the quality-gate ratio flag is EXPECTED, per the roadmap notes.
